sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal; prop. (as I suspect) i.q. גָּרַב (as to the interchange of the letters ג and צ see last remark under , page 698 ), to scratch, to be scabby, rough; whence צָרֶבֶת No. 1.

(2) to be dry, burned up (as to the connection of these significations, see under חָרַר ).

Niphal, to be burned, scorched, Eze 21:3. Kindred are שָׂרַף, סָרַף. Chald. צְרָבָה a burning.

[“צָרָב (for צַדָּב) adj. burning, Pro 16:27 אֵשׁ צָרֶבֶת.”]
